summary: Security-audit-fix verification on LabourLink AI backend. 19/20 pytest tests PASSED (95%). SEC-001 booking state machine ✅ (worker PATCH status='completed' correctly maps to 'completed_by_worker'; only customer can promote to 'completed'; jobs_completed increments ONLY after customer confirms — verified before=0, after=1). SEC-001 withdrawal cap ✅ (amount > balance returns 400 with 'exceeds available balance'; a valid withdrawal < balance succeeded). SEC-002 ✅ (.env JWT_SECRET is 64-char high-entropy, not 'change-in-prod'; decoded fresh token confirms exp - iat ≈ 7.00 days). SEC-003 PARTIAL — /api/workers, /api/workers/popular, /api/workers/{id} all exclude phone & user_id correctly, BUT /api/workers/recommendations STILL LEAKS BOTH 'phone' and 'user_id' (it uses {'_id': 0} projection at server.py:236 instead of PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE). SEC-004 ✅ (duplicate review POST returns 400; unique compound index (booking_id, customer_id) is created in startup; self-onboarded fresh worker has verified=False). SEC-005 ✅ (search='.*' returns 0 workers, confirming _re.escape works). Existing flows verified: register, login, list workers, create booking, worker accept→started→completed_by_worker, customer confirm→completed, single review, favorites add/list/delete, chat send/receive.

critical.issue: SEC-003 FIX INCOMPLETE — endpoint still leaks 'phone' and 'user_id' for every worker in the recommendations array. Root cause: server.py line 236 uses projection `{'_id': 0}` instead of the new `PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE` ({_id:0, phone:0, user_id:0}). Change `db.workers.find(q, {'_id': 0})` to `db.workers.find(q, PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE)`.

action_items: Fix server.py:236 — change `db.workers.find(q, {'_id': 0})` to `db.workers.find(q, PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE)` so /api/workers/recommendations stops leaking phone/user_id. After fix, re-run only: pytest backend/tests/test_security_fixes.py::TestSEC003WorkerPII -v
critical_code_review_comments: Projection inconsistency: 3 of 4 public worker endpoints use PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE; the recommendations endpoint forgot it. Consider a single helper to enforce the public projection.

BookingStatusUpdate Literal allows clients to directly POST status='completed_by_worker'. Worker→customer mapping is the safe path, but a worker could still send status='completed_by_worker' literally. Current state machine `role_allowed` blocks it ('started' → only {'completed_by_worker'} for worker AND `effective_new` is unchanged because requested_role is worker and new_status != 'completed' → effective_new stays 'completed_by_worker' → allowed). So actually it's fine, but verify intent.
Withdrawal balance check now correctly recomputes server-side (sum(completed) - sum(pending+completed withdrawals)). Good.
Reviews unique index requires fresh DB or non-existent prior conflicting rows; if old data has duplicates, startup index creation will fail silently. Verified startup succeeded here.
server.py now 728 lines — exceeds the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into modules.
seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed was idempotent ('already seeded'). One fresh worker user created per session: TEST_worker_<uuid>@example.com / Worker@123 — onboarded as plumber, completed full booking lifecycle with demo customer, received 1 five-star review, performed one withdrawal of ₹100. No destructive cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Only failing case is TestSEC003WorkerPII::test_recommendations_no_pii. Apply the one-line fix in server.py:236 (use PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE), restart backend (`sudo supervisorctl restart backend`), then re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_security_fixes.py -v`. All other security findings are correctly addressed.
rca_of_issue: RCA — SEC-003 leak in /api/workers/recommendations: The fix introduced a `PUBLIC_WORKER_EXCLUDE` projection constant (server.py:29) and applied it to /api/workers (line 223), /api/workers/popular (line 229), and /api/workers/{id} (line 273). The recommendations endpoint (line 236) was missed — it still uses the older `{'_id': 0}` projection, so `phone` and `user_id` flow through. Reproduction: POST /api/seed; POST /api/auth/login (demo creds); GET /api/workers/recommendations?category=plumber with Bearer token → response.recommendations[*] contains both 'phone' and 'user_id'. Mitigation: one-line projection change as noted in action_items.
